mnemonics: Stars and Bars Visual: Imagine distributing identical items (stars) into distinct categories separated by bars. For n items and k categories, you need k-1 bars. The formula C(n + k - 1, k - 1) or C(n + k - 1, n) becomes intuitive when you see that you're arranging n stars and k-1 bars together.
keyPoints: Combinations with repetition count multisets of size n from k types: order doesn't matter, repetition is allowed.
The standard formula is C(n + k - 1, n) or equivalently C(n + k - 1, k - 1).
This is equivalent to the number of non-negative integer solutions to the equation x₁ + x₂ + ... + xₖ = n.
commonMistakes: Confusing combinations with repetition with standard combinations (which forbid repetition).
Forgetting that the formula is for non-negative integers (including zero as a possible count for an item type).
Mistakenly using permutations with repetition (where order matters) instead of combinations (where order does not matter).
